name: Self-join hierarchical probe (post DropUnresolvedSelectColumnsRule)
description: 5 cases targeting the SQL-error pattern from baseline 190. 3 are the self-join 'X with its parent X' shape that hit 'multi-part identifier could not be bound'. 2 are control cases (label-projection regression check + a basic SEL) to ensure the new rule doesn't drop legitimate projections.

Scenarios.Question: list every region with its parent region
Scenarios.Notes: From B-SEL-009. The LLM projects 'ParentRegion.NameEn' without adding the self-join. Our rule should drop that entry; SQL becomes Regions.NameEn only (partial but correct).

Scenarios.Question: regions and their parent governorate
Scenarios.Notes: From B-JOI-009. Same shape as 001 with different alias name (ParentGovernorate).

Scenarios.Question: show all ticket categories with their parent category code
Scenarios.Notes: From B-CTE-003. The LLM used aliases 'tc' and 'tc2' which were never bound. Drop unresolved.

Scenarios.Question: top 5 customers by total bill amount
Scenarios.Notes: Control case — exercises the ProjectLabelForFkGroupByRule. Should still produce Customers.FullNameEn + SUM. Verifies new rule doesn't break the label-projection fix.

Scenarios.Question: show me tickets
Scenarios.Notes: Control — basic SEL with FK label projections. Verifies the new rule doesn't drop legitimate FK label projections that the compiler will auto-join.
